title: POST /api/webhooks/resend — bounce/complaint events from Resend
problem: Resend fires webhooks when a recipient's mail server tells it something meaningful about a previously-delivered message: hard bounce, soft bounce, spam complaint, scheduled-delivery delayed, etc. Without ingesting these we keep sending magic-link / tier-assigned / refund emails to addresses the rest of the internet has decided are dead — burning sender reputation along the way. This handler is the ingest endpoint: it verifies Resend's Svix-style signature, dedupes via the shared webhook_events table, and writes a row per bounce/complaint/delivered/delayed/opened/clicked into email_events. Code-only landing: P1.1 (custom-domain DNS) gates real events flowing in, so the handler stays inert (503 while RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset).
analysis: POST only. Auth: Svix-style HMAC-SHA-256 over `${svix-id}.${svix-timestamp}.${raw-body}` using RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET (whsec_ prefix stripped if present). 5-minute timestamp tolerance to prevent replay. Constant-time signature compare. Header `svix-signature` is a space-separated list of `<version>,<sig>` pairs — accept on any v1 match (rotated keys). CSRF-exempt in middleware. Idempotency: INSERT OR IGNORE into webhook_events keyed on the Svix event id; duplicate returns 200 + duplicate=true with no email_events row. Informational `email.sent` is accepted (dedupe row) but dropped (no email_events row). Anything in INGEST_TYPES (delivered, delivery_delayed, bounced, complained, opened, clicked) writes a row carrying the recipient + type + subtype (bounce_type / reason / subtype field extracted from the data payload) + raw payload TEXT (truncated to 16KB defensively). 503 when secret unset, 401 on signature mismatch, 200 on every other outcome so Resend doesn't retry into backoff.

decisions.decision: Land the handler before activation (DNS-blocked) instead of waiting
decisions.rationale: The handler is the longest part of the work; DNS records + dashboard secret deploy are 5 minutes total once DNS access is available. Shipping inert lets the activation be a single-sitting flip without a code change. The 503 shape while RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset means a stray request in the meantime is a non-event.
decisions.recommendation: If Resend ever changes its webhook signing scheme away from Svix, the verifier is the only piece to swap — the dedupe + ingest + 503-graceful-degrade structure stays.

decisions.decision: Reuse webhook_events table for dedupe instead of a per-source table
decisions.rationale: Stripe events are evt_..., Resend events are msg_... — no namespace collision in practice. One table = one INSERT OR IGNORE pattern that future webhook sources (CF Email Routing, support inbox?) plug into without schema work.
decisions.recommendation: If a third webhook source ever shares an id namespace with one of these, add a 'source' column. Not worth doing pre-emptively.

decisions.decision: Persist the raw payload TEXT, not just a structured projection
decisions.rationale: Resend's payload shape varies per event type and per version; modeling every field at the schema level would mean migration on every Resend release-note bump. Keep `type`, `subtype`, `to_email`, `received_at` as queryable columns (the dimensions an operator actually filters on) and stash the rest as TEXT for forensics. ~1 KB per row.
decisions.recommendation: If a specific field becomes load-bearing (e.g. bounce category drives an automatic suppression-list update), promote it to a column then. Premature normalization is the trap.

decisions.decision: Drop email.sent events without an email_events row
decisions.rationale: email.sent is just Resend confirming the API call landed — which we already know from send()'s success return. Storing a row per outbound email would inflate the table for zero forensic value; the durable email_outbox table already records the rows that mattered to begin with.
decisions.recommendation: If we ever want a full outbound-mail audit trail, that's a separate table (mail_log), not this one.

successCriteria:
- 503 when RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET unset (graceful degrade)
- 401 on signature mismatch (Resend will not retry)
- 200 + dedupe on duplicate event ids (Resend retry storm absorbed)
- 200 + email_events row written on bounce/complaint/delivered/delayed/opened/clicked
- 200 + ignored=true for informational email.sent (no row)
- Payload TEXT truncated to 16KB defensively
- Svix timestamp tolerance window enforced (5 minutes) — replay attack rejected
- Rotated multi-sig header accepted via any-match
implementation: functions/api/webhooks/resend.ts — onRequestPost. Verifier in functions/_lib/resendWebhook.ts (Svix HMAC, constant-time compare, recipientOf normalizer for string-or-array `to`). Storage migration: 0027_email_events.sql with two indexes (per-recipient, recent-first). CSRF exemption added to functions/_middleware.ts. Tests: functions/_lib/resendWebhook.test.ts (10 cases — verifier in isolation) + functions/api/webhooks/resend.test.ts (5 cases — handler integration).
userGuide: Activate via RUNBOOK §16: add marcportal.com on Resend Dashboard, add the 4 DNS records to Cloudflare, wait for verification, configure the webhook endpoint at https://marcportal.com/api/webhooks/resend with bounce + complaint + delivered events, `wrangler secret put RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. To inspect events: `SELECT * FROM email_events ORDER BY received_at DESC LIMIT 20`. To find bouncing addresses: `SELECT to_email, COUNT(*) FROM email_events WHERE type = 'email.bounced' GROUP BY to_email`.
knownLimitations: Dormant until P1.1 (custom-domain DNS) is configured — no real events flow before then
No automatic suppression of bouncing addresses at send time yet; future work once real bounce data accumulates
Svix signature scheme is hard-coded; a future Resend rework to a different signer would need code change in resendWebhook.ts
Subtype extraction is naive — checks bounce_type / reason / subtype keys. New event types with different subtype fields land NULL until added
email.sent dedupes (occupies a webhook_events row) but doesn't write to email_events; minor wastage on the dedupe table

statusHistory.reason: Code-only landing. Stays draft until P1.1 DNS is live and the first real event ingests successfully — at which point it advances to active.

P1.1 confirmed already done (domain verified 2026-05-16). RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET deployed via wrangler pages secret put; webhook endpoint configured on Resend Dashboard. Awaiting first real ingest to advance to frozen.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial author for the new webhook endpoint (AUDIT P1.2 code-only landing).
